theorem.class9.product_log.title: Product Law of Logarithms
theorem.class9.change_base.step1_detail: 📱 Aap ek calculator program kar rahay hain jisme sirf log₁₀ (common log) aur ln (natural log) buttons hain, lekin aapko log₂(8) compute karna hai. Yeh wo classic problem hai jo Change of Base Law solve karta hai.
theorem.class9.change_base.step2_detail: Convert karein: m = b^x. Hum isko ek known base a ki terms mein likhna chahte hain. Dono sides ka log_a lenay se: log_a m = log_a(b^x). Ab hum Power Law ko reverse mein use kar saktay hain!
theorem.class9.change_base.step3_detail: log_a m = x · log_a b. Exponent x samnay "pull down" ho jata hai. Ab x ko isolate karein: x = log_a m / log_a b.
theorem.class9.change_base.step4_detail: Kyunke x = log_b m, humein milta hai: log_b m = log_a m / log_a b. Toh log₂(8) = log₁₀(8) / log₁₀(2) = 0.9031 / 0.3010 = 3. Wakai, 2³ = 8.
theorem.class9.change_base.practice_ques: log₁₀(100) = 2. Change of base use kar ke log₂(100) find karein.
Equal chords → centre se equal distances.
Equal chords centre se equidistant hotay hain.
Equal chords centre se equidistant hotay hain
theorem.class10.equidistant_chords.title: Theorem 9.5: Equidistant Chords Congruent hotay hain
theorem.class10.equidistant_chords.final: Chords jo centre se equal distance par hon, unki length equal hoti hai.
theorem.class10.equidistant_chords.keyIn: Yeh "if and only if" ko complete karta hai: chords equal hain ↔ equidistant hain. Yeh symmetry circular grid design aur radial structures jaise Ferris wheels mein fundamental hai.
theorem.class10.equidistant_chords.step1: Equal distance par chords draw karein
🎪 Do chords AB aur CD draw karein is tarah ke centre se unke perpendicular distances (OE aur OF) equal hon.
theorem.class10.tangent_perp_radius.step: ⭕ Centre O ke sath ek circle draw karein. Ek tangent PQ draw karein jo circle ko point B par touch kare. Radius OB draw karein.
Agar OB perpendicular NAHI hai, toh PQ par koi aur point B ki nisbat O ke zyada qareeb hoga.
Wo qareeb wala point circle ke andar hoga, jiska matlab hai ke PQ circle ko do points par intersect karega — jo is baat ko contradict karta hai ke PQ ek tangent hai.
Assumption galat hai, isliye OB ⟂ PQ. Radius aur tangent hamesha perpendicular hotay hain.
theorem.class10.tangent_perp_radius.prac: Radius OB tangent ko B par milta hai. Unke darmiyan angle kya hai?
Tangent point of contact par radius ke perpendicular hota hai.
theorem.class10.equal_tangents.title: Theorem 10.3: External Point se Do Tangents Equal hotay hain
theorem.class10.equal_tangents.finalForm: Ek external point se circle par draw kiye gaye do tangents ki lengths equal hoti hain.
theorem.class10.internal_touching.step1_: ⭕🔵 Ek bada circle (radius r₁) draw karein aur ek chota circle (radius r₂) uske andar draw karein, jo C par touch kare.
theorem.class10.internal_touching.step2_: Circles C par touch karte hain, jo line AB par lie karta hai. Chota circle bade circle ke andar hai.
theorem.class10.internal_touching.step3_: Outer circle ke liye: AC = r₁. Inner circle ke liye: BC = r₂. Kyunke B andar hai: AB = r₁ − r₂.
theorem.class10.internal_touching.practi: Do circles jinke radii 5 cm aur 2 cm hain, internally touch karte hain. Centres ke darmiyan distance kya hoga?
theorem.class10.alternate_segment.title: Theorem 10.6: Alternate Segment Theorem
theorem.class10.alternate_segment.finalF: Tangent aur chord ke darmiyan angle, alternate segment mein angle ke barabar hota hai.
theorem.class10.alternate_segment.keyIns: Sailors is theorem ko use karte hain: ek coastline (tangent) aur lighthouse ki line (chord) ke darmiyan angle unki position determine karta hai. Yeh circle geometry ke sabse khubsurat theorems mein se ek hai!
theorem.class10.alternate_segment.step1_: 🎯 Ek circle draw karein jiska tangent PT point A par ho. Chord AB draw karein, jo tangent aur chord ke darmiyan ∠APT create kare.
theorem.class10.alternate_segment.step2_: Chord AB circle ko do segments mein divide karta hai. Alternate segment tangent ke opposite hota hai.
theorem.class10.alternate_segment.step3_: Tangent aur chord ke darmiyan angle alternate segment mein angle ke barabar hota hai.
theorem.class10.major_segment.title: Theorem 10.10: Major Segment mein Angle < 90°
theorem.class10.major_segment.finalFormu: Ek segment jo semicircle se bada ho, usme angle acute hota hai.
theorem.class10.major_segment.keyInsight: Theorems 10.11 ke sath mil kar, yeh circular segments mein angles ki complete classification deta hai. Semicircle 90° par acute aur obtuse ke darmiyan boundary hai.
theorem.class10.major_segment.step1_labe: Chord AB draw karein jo major segment banaye
theorem.class10.major_segment.step1_deta: 📐 Chord AB draw karein jo ek major segment (semicircle se bada) create kare. Major arc par ek point P pick karein.
theorem.class10.major_segment.step2_deta: Major segment se banne wala central angle 180° se kam hota hai.
theorem.class10.major_segment.step3_deta: Kyunke central angle 180° se kam hai, inscribed angle 90° se kam (acute) hota hai.
theorem.class11.complex_magnitude.title: Complex Number ka Magnitude
theorem.class11.complex_magnitude.finalF: z = x + iy ka magnitude complex plane mein origin se distance hota hai.
theorem.class11.complex_magnitude.keyIns: Magnitude |z| ko modulus ya absolute value bhi kaha jata hai. Yeh measure karta hai ke complex number z = x + iy complex plane par origin se kitna door hai, bilkul distance formula ki tarah! Yeh signal processing mein fundamental hai jahan |z| ek signal ka amplitude represent karta hai.
theorem.class11.complex_magnitude.step1_: 🗺️ Aap ek radar operator hain jo ek circular radar screen par plane ko track kar rahay hain. Plane ki position (x, y) par hai — real part x east-west coordinate hai, aur imaginary part y north-south coordinate hai. Centre (origin) se distance range ke liye ahem hai.
theorem.class11.complex_inverse_polar.pr: |z⁻¹| = 1/2. Inverse ka magnitude reciprocal hota hai.
theorem.class11.complex_division_polar.t: Polar Form mein Complex Numbers ki Division
theorem.class11.complex_division_polar.f: Magnitudes ko divide karein, angles ko subtract karein.
theorem.class11.complex_division_polar.k: Polar form mein complex division bohot elegant hai: magnitudes ko divide karein aur angles ko subtract karein! Yeh rectangular form se kahin zyada aasan hai aur AC power systems mein phasor analysis ke liye bohot zyada use hota hai.
theorem.class11.complex_division_polar.s: Numerator aur denominator ko multiply karein
Trig identities use kar ke simplify karein

🔌 Ek electrical engineer power grid analyze kar raha hai. Do AC voltages z₁ = r₁(cos θ₁ + i sin θ₁) aur z₂ = r₂(cos θ₂ + i sin θ₂) hain. Unki ratio find karne ke liye, unhein polar form mein divide karein.
theorem.class11.determinant_identical.st: 📉 Aapka GPS receiver aapki position triangulate karne ke liye teen satellite signals (rows) use karta hai. Agar do satellites identical signals bhejein (identical rows), toh receiver unko distinguish nahi kar sakta — yeh ek unique location find nahi kar sakta!

🎯 Agar hum Row 1 aur Row 2 swap karein, kyunke woh identical hain, matrix B bilkul matrix A jaisa hoga. Lekin interchange property ke mutabiq, rows swap karne se determinant ka sign flip hota hai: |B| = −|A|.
🧮 Kyunke B = A (identical rows), |B| = |A|. Lekin ye bhi ke |B| = −|A| (interchange property se). Isliye |A| = −|A|.

✅ Dono sides par |A| add karne se: 2|A| = 0 → |A| = 0. Yehi wajah hai ke Gauss-Jordan elimination tab toot jata hai jab rows dependent hon — determinant zero hona humein batata hai ke matrix singular hai!
theorem.class11.determinant_identical.pr: Ek 3×3 matrix ki first aur second rows identical hain. det(A) kya hoga?
Identical rows → determinant zaroor zero hoga
theorem.class11.trapezium_midpoint.title: Trapezium Midpoint Theorem
theorem.class11.trapezium_midpoint.final: Non-parallel sides ke midpoints ko jorne wala segment parallel sides ke sum ka half hota hai.
theorem.class11.trapezium_midpoint.keyIn: Bridge designers is theorem ko use karte hain: ek trapezoidal bridge girder ki midline top aur bottom widths ki average ke barabar hoti hai. Yeh numerical integration (trapezoidal rule) mein bhi use hota hai jahan endpoints ki average integral ko approximate karti hai!
theorem.class11.trapezium_midpoint.step1: 🌉 Aap ek civil engineer hain jo ek bridge design kar rahay hain jiski cross-section trapezoidal hai. Top deck AB, bottom base DC ke parallel hai. Aapko mid-span support beam ki length find karni hai.
theorem.class11.combination_formula.step: Farz karein X = number of combinations

🎯 Aap 5 volunteers mein se 3-person team organize kar rahay hain. Aap pehle permutations (ordered arrangements) ke baray mein sochte hain, lekin ehsaas hota hai ke order matter nahi karta — Alice, Bob, Charlie ko pick karna aur Charlie, Bob, Alice ko pick karna ek hi team hai.
📐 r objects ki har combination ko r! different tarikon se arrange kiya ja sakta hai. Isliye number of permutations (ordered) = number of combinations × arrangements: ⁿPᵣ = X × r!.
🧮 Humein pata hai ⁿPᵣ = n!/(n−r)!. Substitute karne par: X × r! = n!/(n−r)!.

✅ Dono sides ko r! se divide karein: X = ⁿCᵣ = n!/(r!(n−r)!). 5-choose-3 ke liye: ⁵C₃ = 5!/(3!2!) = 120/(6×2) = 10. 5 logon mein se 10 possible 3-person teams ban sakti hain. Yeh binomial distribution aur Pascal's Triangle ki foundation hai!
theorem.class11.binomial_theorem.step2_d: 📐 Farz karein theorem n = k ke liye theek hai: (a+b)ᵏ = Σ ᵏCᵢ aᵏ⁻ⁱ bⁱ.
theorem.class11.binomial_theorem.step3_d: 🧮 k+1 ke liye prove karne ke liye, dono sides ko (a+b) se multiply karein: (a+b)ᵏ⁺¹ = (a+b)(ᵏC₀aᵏ + ᵏC₁aᵏ⁻¹b + ... + ᵏCₖbᵏ).
theorem.class11.binomial_theorem.step4_d: ✅ Distribute aur like terms ko group karne ke baad, coefficients Pascal ki identity follow karte hain: ᵏCᵣ₋₁ + ᵏCᵣ = ᵏ⁺¹Cᵣ. Isliye (a+b)ᵏ⁺¹ = Σ ᵏ⁺¹Cᵣ aᵏ⁺¹⁻ʳ bʳ. Induction ke zariye, theorem sabhi positive integers n ke liye hold karta hai!
theorem.class11.binomial_theorem.practic: (a+b)⁴ mein a²b² ka coefficient find karein.
Pascal's triangle ki Row 4: 1, 4, 6, 4, 1. Coefficient a²b² ka = ⁴C₂ = 6
Pascal's Triangle row n, position k use karein
theorem.class11.fundamental_trig.title: Fundamental Law of Trigonometry: cos(α−β)
theorem.class11.fundamental_trig.finalFo: Difference ka cosine, cosines ke product aur sines ke product ke sum ke barabar hota hai.
theorem.class11.fundamental_trig.keyInsi: Yeh single formula tamam trigonometric identities ki maa hai! Is se hum cos(α+β), sin(α±β), double-angle formulas, half-angle formulas, aur product-to-sum identities derive kar saktay hain. Yeh trigonometry ka Rosetta Stone hai.
theorem.class11.trig_identities.step2_de: 🔄 Co-ratios use karte hue: sin(α+β) = cos(π/2−(α+β)) = cos[(π/2−α)−β] = cos(π/2−α)cos β + sin(π/2−α)sin β = sin α cos β + cos α sin β.
theorem.class11.trig_identities.step3_de: 🧮 sin(α+β) mein β = α letay hain: sin(2α) = sin α cos α + cos α sin α = 2 sin α cos α. Cos ke liye: cos(2α) = cos²α − sin²α = 2cos²α − 1 = 1 − 2sin²α.
theorem.class11.trig_identities.step4_de: 🔊 Audio engineers isko use karte hain: sin(3α) = sin(2α+α) = sin(2α)cos α + cos(2α)sin α = (2 sin α cos α)cos α + (1 − 2 sin²α)sin α = 2 sin α(1−sin²α) + sin α − 2 sin³α = 3 sin α − 4 sin³α. Yeh synthesizers mein third harmonic generate karta hai!
theorem.class11.trig_identities.practice: sin(30°) = 0.5. Double-angle formula use karke sin(60°) find karein.
theorem.class12.power_rule.title: Power Rule for Differentiation
theorem.class12.power_rule.finalFormulaD: Kisi bhi positive integer n ke liye xⁿ ka derivative n·xⁿ⁻¹ hota hai.
theorem.class12.power_rule.keyInsight: Power rule sab se zyada use hone wala differentiation formula hai! Yeh polynomials ko simple bana deta hai: har term ka exponent 1 se kam ho kar naya coefficient ban jata hai. Yeh physics (velocity = derivative of position) se lekar economics (total cost se marginal cost) tak har jagah use hota hai.
theorem.class12.substitution_integrals.s: 📐 Ek astronomer radius a ke circular telescope mirror ka area calculate kar raha hai. Integral ∫ √(a²−x²) dx aata hai. Substitute x = a sin θ, jo circle ki equation ko trigonometric form mein convert kar deta hai.
🧮 √(a²−x²) = √(a²−a²sin²θ) = a√(1−sin²θ) = a cos θ. Integral ye ban jata hai ∫ a cos θ · a cos θ dθ = a² ∫ cos²θ dθ.
📐 cos²θ = (1+cos 2θ)/2 use karein. Integrate: a²[θ/2 + sin 2θ/4] + C = (a²/2)θ + (a²/4) sin 2θ + C.

✅ Wapas substitute karne se: ∫√(a²−x²)dx = (a²/2)sin⁻¹(x/a) + (x√(a²−x²))/2 + C. Yeh formula height x ke circular segment ka area deta hai — civil engineering mein partially bhari pipes mein pani ke flow ke liye use hota hai!
theorem.class12.substitution_integrals.p: Formula ∫ 1/√(a²−x²) dx = sin⁻¹(x/a) + C use karein
theorem.class12.fundamental_theorem_calc: Definite integral bounds par antiderivative ke difference ke barabar hota hai.

FTC calculus ka sab se ahem theorem hai! Yeh do central concepts ko milata hai: differentiation aur integration inverse operations hain. Yehi wajah hai ke hum antiderivatives maloom kar ke area nikal saktay hain — geometry aur algebra ke darmiyan ek profound link.

🏔️ Ek geologist river delta mein sediment ka volume calculate kar raha hai. Cross-sectional area shore se distance x ke sath vary karta hai. Define A(x) = ∫ₐˣ f(t) dt as the accumulated area a se x tak.
📐 Definition ke mutabiq: A'(x) = lim[A(x+h)−A(x)]/h as h→0. Lekin A(x+h)−A(x) = ∫ₓˣ⁺ʰ f(t) dt ≈ f(x)·h Mean Value Theorem ke zariye. So A'(x) = f(x). Area function f ka ek antiderivative hai!
🧮 Kyunke A'(x) = f(x), f ka koi bhi antiderivative F A se ek constant se differ karta hai: F(x) = A(x) + C. Toh F(b)−F(a) = A(b)−A(a).

✅ Lekin A(a) = ∫ₐᵃ f(t) dt = 0, isliye F(b)−F(a) = A(b) = ∫ₐᵇ f(x) dx. Definite integral bounds par evaluate kiye gaye antiderivative ke barabar hota hai. River delta volume: agar A'(x) = 2x (linear increase), volume x=0 se x=5 tak = [x²]₀⁵ = 25 cubic meters!
∫₁³ (2x) dx. Find F(3) − F(1) jahan F(x) = x².
theorem.class12.concurrency_altitudes.fi: Triangle ke teeno altitudes hamesha ek hi point (orthocenter) par intersect karte hain.
theorem.class12.concurrency_altitudes.ke: Orthocenter tamam teen altitudes ka intersection hai. Ek acute triangle mein yeh andar hota hai; obtuse triangle mein yeh bahar hota hai! Yeh concurrency wo wajah hai ke triangle ke teeno altitudes hamesha miltay hain — ek shandar geometric haqeeqat jo surveying mein positions locate karne ke liye use hoti hai.
theorem.class12.concurrency_altitudes.st: 🏔️ Ek surveyor zameen ke triangular plot ko map kar raha hai. Orthocenter find karne ke liye (jahan elevation ki sab lines milti hain), unhein prove karna hoga ke teeno altitudes ek point par intersect karti hain.
📐 BC ki slope (y₃−y₂)/(x₃−x₂) hai. Altitude AD ∦ BC, isliye iski slope negative reciprocal hai: m_AD = −(x₃−x₂)/(y₃−y₂).
🧮 Point A(x₁, y₁) ke sath point-slope form use karte hue: y − y₁ = m_AD(x − x₁). Rearrange karein: (x₃−x₂)x + (y₃−y₂)y − x₁(x₃−x₂) − y₁(y₃−y₂) = 0.

✅ BE aur CF ke liye similar equations likhein. Unke coefficients ek determinant mein rakhein. R₂ + R₃ ko R₁ mein add karne se first row zero ho jati hai (kyunke har row se coefficients ka sum cancel ho jata hai). Zero row ka matlab determinant = 0, jo prove karta hai ke lines orthocenter par concurrent hain!
theorem.class12.concurrency_altitudes.pr: Triangle jiske vertices (0,0), (4,0), (2,5) hain. Teeno altitudes ek single point par milti hain. Is property ko kya kehte hain?
Hint: Yeh "con" se shuru hota hai aur lines of elevation se relate karta hai.
Sochein ke teen lines ke ek point par milne ka kya matlab hai
theorem.class12.concurrency_bisectors.fi: Triangle ki sides ke perpendicular bisectors circumcenter par intersect karte hain.
theorem.class12.concurrency_bisectors.ke: Circumcenter us circle ka center hai jo teeno vertices se guzarta hai. Yeh teen perpendicular bisectors ka intersection hai. Yeh point sabhi vertices se equidistant hota hai — jo explain karta hai ke aap hamesha kisi bhi teen non-collinear points se ek unique circle draw kar sakte hain!
theorem.class12.concurrency_bisectors.st: Simplify karein aur standard form mein likhein

📍 GPS triangulation: Teen satellite positions A, B, C known hain. Apni location find karne ke liye, AB aur BC ke perpendicular bisectors construct karein — unka intersection aapki position (circumcenter) hai!
📐 BC ka right bisector midpoint se guzarta hai jiski slope BC ke perpendicular hoti hai.
🧮 Through multiply karne aur rearrange karne se standard linear equation milti hai. Note karein ke constant terms mein coordinates ke squares shamil hain.

✅ Teeno bisectors ke liye equations likhein. R₂ + R₃ ko add karne ke baad coefficient determinant ki first row zero ho jati hai. Zero determinant = concurrency. Intersection point (circumcenter) teeno vertices se equidistant hai — circumscribed circle ka center!
theorem.class12.concurrency_bisectors.pr: Circumscribed circle ke center ko kya kaha jata hai?
theorem.class12.concurrency_medians.fina: Triangle ke teeno medians centroid par intersect karte hain — triangle ka center of mass.
theorem.class12.concurrency_medians.keyI: Centroid har median ko vertex se midpoint tak 2:1 ratio mein divide karta hai. Yeh triangle ka center of mass hai — agar aap cardboard se ek triangle kaatain, toh yeh centroid par perfectly balance hoga! Engineers isko triangular structures ka center of gravity find karne ke liye use karte hain.
theorem.class12.concurrency_medians.step: Median AD: vertex A se BC ke midpoint tak

⚖️ Ek architect bridge ki roof ke liye ek triangular steel truss design kar raha hai. Center of mass (centroid) wahan hota hai jahan medians intersect karte hain — yahan par main support column rakha jana chahiye.
📐 Median AD A(x₁, y₁) aur BC ke midpoint se guzarta hai. Two-point formula use karke.
🧮 Cross-multiply karein aur ek standard linear form hasil karne ke liye simplify karein.

✅ Teeno median equations likhein. Rows add karne ke baad unka coefficient determinant zero ho jata hai, jo concurrency prove karta hai. Intersection point G = ((x₁+x₂+x₃)/3, (y₁+y₂+y₃)/3) — centroid. Yeh vertices ki average bhi hai, jo isay center of mass banata hai!
theorem.class12.concurrency_medians.prac: Triangle jiske vertices (0,0), (6,0), (3,6) hain. Centroid x-coordinate find karein.
theorem.class12.homogeneous_second_degre: Har homogeneous second-degree equation origin se guzarne wali do lines represent karti hai.

Ek homogeneous equation ax² + 2hxy + by² = 0 mein tamam terms ki degree 2 hoti hai. Yeh hamesha do linear equations mein factor hoti hai, jiska matlab hai yeh origin se guzarne wali do straight lines represent karti hai. Agar h² − ab > 0, toh lines distinct aur real hoti hain — jo optics mein light cone sections analyze karne ke liye use hoti hai!
Dono sides ko a se multiply karein
Difference of squares ke tor par factor karein

🔦 Ek physicist point source se phailnay wali light ki beam analyze kar raha hai. Light cone ek homogeneous equation se describe hota hai: ax² + 2hxy + by² = 0. Maqsad isko do distinct rays mein factor karna hai.
📐 Key insight: h²y² add aur subtract kar ke perfect square complete karein.
🧮 Pehli teen terms (ax + hy)² banati hain. Baqi terms −y²(h²−ab) ban jati hain.

✅ Difference of squares ke tor par: (ax + hy)² − (y√(h²−ab))² = 0. Yeh do linear equations mein factor hota hai: ax + y(h ± √(h²−ab)) = 0. Dono origin se guzarti hain (koi constant term nahi). Agar h² > ab: do distinct real lines. Agar h² = ab: ek repeated line. Agar h² < ab: complex (imaginary) lines.
Kya x² − y² = 0 do lines mein factor hoti hai?
x² − y² = (x−y)(x+y) = 0 se lines x=y aur x=−y milti hain
Haan! x² − y² = (x−y)(x+y) = 0 origin se guzarne wali do lines hain: x = y aur x = −y.
theorem.class12.circle_general.title: Circle ki General Equation
theorem.class12.circle_general.finalForm: General equation ek circle represent karti hai jiska center (−g, −f) aur radius √(g²+f²−c) hota hai.
theorem.class12.circle_general.keyInsigh: Square complete kar ke, kisi bhi equation x² + y² + 2gx + 2fy + c = 0 ko circle ke tor par likha ja sakta hai. Agar g²+f²−c > 0, toh yeh ek real circle hai; agar 0 ke barabar ho, toh yeh ek point circle hai; agar negative ho, toh equation ka koi real graph nahi hota. Is tarah GPS receivers teen circle equations se aapki position determine karte hain!
theorem.class12.circle_general.step2_lab: x aur y terms ko group karein
theorem.class12.circle_general.step3_lab: x aur y ke liye square complete karein
theorem.class12.circle_general.step1_det: 🗺️ Ek GPS receiver teen satellites se signals detect karta hai. Har satellite ka signal ek circle define karta hai: x² + y² + 2gx + 2fy + c = 0. Receiver ko aapki position triangulate karne ke liye har circle ka center aur radius find karna hota hai.
theorem.class12.circle_general.step2_det: 📐 x aur y terms ko separate karein, c ko right side par le jayen.
theorem.class12.circle_general.step3_det: 🧮 g² add karein (ta ke (x+g)² complete ho) aur f² add karein (ta ke (y+f)² complete ho). Balance karne ke liye same side par g² aur f² subtract karein.
theorem.class12.circle_general.step4_det: ✅ Yeh standard circle form (x−h)² + (y−k)² = r² se match karta hai jahan h = −g, k = −f, r = √(g²+f²−c). Center (−g, −f) aur radius √(g²+f²−c) hai. Ek real circle ke liye, humein g²+f²−c > 0 chahiye!
theorem.class12.circle_general.practice_: x²+y²+4x+6y+4=0. Center ka x-coordinate find karein.
theorem.class12.circle_tangent.title: Kisi Diye Gaye Point par Circle par Tangent
theorem.class12.circle_tangent.finalForm: Point P(x₁, y₁) par circle ke tangent ki equation.
theorem.class12.circle_tangent.keyInsigh: Kisi point par circle ka tangent us point par radius ke perpendicular hota hai. Formula x₁x + y₁y + g(x + x₁) + f(y + y₁) + c = 0 slope condition apply kar ke derive kiya jata hai. Ise "T = 0" ke naam se jana jata hai — x² → x₁x, y² → y₁y, x → (x+x₁)/2, y → (y+y₁)/2 replace karein!
theorem.class12.circle_tangent.step1_lab: Implicit differentiation se slope find karein
theorem.class12.circle_tangent.step3_lab: Point P(x₁, y₁) par point-slope form
theorem.class12.circle_tangent.step1_det: 🎯 Ek machinist ko gear tooth katna hai jo circular gear par ek precise point par contact banaye. Cutting tool ko contact point par tangent line follow karni chahiye.
theorem.class12.circle_tangent.step2_det: 📐 Implicitly differentiate karein: 2x + 2y·y' + 2g + 2f·y' = 0. y' ke liye solve karein: dy/dx = −(x+g)/(y+f).
theorem.class12.circle_tangent.step3_det: 🧮 Point P(x₁, y₁) par, dy/dx mein substitute karein: slope m = −(x₁+g)/(y₁+f). Point-slope form mein tangent line: y − y₁ = m(x − x₁).
theorem.class12.circle_tangent.step4_det: ✅ Cross-multiply aur rearrange karein. Result elegant T = 0 formula hai: replace x² → x₁x, y² → y₁y, 2gx → g(x+x₁), 2fy → f(y+y₁), c → c. Tangent equation derivative ko explicitly compute kiye baghair likhi jati hai!
theorem.class12.circle_tangent.practice_: Circle x²+y²=25 point (3,4) par. Tangent slope = −x₁/y₁ = −3/4 = ?
theorem.class12.parabola_standard.title: Parabola ki Standard Equation
theorem.class12.parabola_standard.finalF: Ek parabola jiska focus (a, 0) aur directrix x = −a par hai.
theorem.class12.parabola_standard.keyIns: Parabola un points ka set hai jo ek fixed point (focus) aur ek fixed line (directrix) se equidistant hotay hain. Is reflective property ki wajah se parabolic mirrors parallel light rays ko focus par concentrate karte hain — jo telescopes, satellite dishes, aur solar cookers mein use hote hain!
theorem.class12.parabola_standard.step1_: Definition: focus ka distance = directrix ka distance
theorem.class12.parabola_standard.step2_: Focus ko (a, 0) aur directrix ko x = −a par rakhein
theorem.class12.parabola_standard.step1_: 📡 Ek satellite dish antenna parabolic shape ka hota hai. Axis ke parallel aanay walay signals focus par reflect hotay hain jahan receiver rakha jata hai. Definition: parabola par har point P focus F aur directrix line se equidistant hota hai.
theorem.class12.parabola_standard.step2_: 📐 Coordinate axes choose karein ta ke focus F = (a, 0) aur directrix x = −a ho. P(x,y) se F ka distance √[(x−a)²+y²] hai. Directrix ka distance |x−(−a)| = |x + a| hai.
theorem.class12.parabola_standard.step3_: 🧮 Dono sides ka square lein (dono non-negative hain): (x−a)² + y² = (x+a)².
theorem.class12.parabola_standard.step4_: ✅ Expand karein: x²−2ax+a²+y² = x²+2ax+a². Dono sides par x² aur a² ko cancel karein. Solve karein: y² = 4ax. Yeh right ki taraf open hone walay parabola ki standard equation hai. Satellite dishes ke liye: receiver focus (a, 0) par hota hai aur dish y² = 4ax ko follow karti hai!
theorem.class12.parabola_standard.practi: y² = 4ax. 4a = coefficient se a ke liye solve karein.
theorem.class12.parabola_tangent.title: Parabola par Tangent
theorem.class12.parabola_tangent.finalFo: Tangency ke liye shart: y = mx + c ko y² = 4ax mein substitute karein aur discriminant = 0 set karein.
theorem.class12.parabola_tangent.keyInsi: Line y = mx + c parabola y² = 4ax par tangent hoti hai agar c = a/m ho. Iska matlab tangent ki slope form y = mx + a/m hai. Kisi diye gaye slope m ke liye, theek do parallel tangents hotay hain (parabola ki har side par ek), siwaye vertex par vertical tangent ke!
theorem.class12.parabola_tangent.step1_l: Line ko parabola mein substitute karein
theorem.class12.parabola_tangent.step2_l: x mein quadratic form mein expand karein
theorem.class12.parabola_tangent.step3_l: Tangency ke liye discriminant = 0 set karein
theorem.class12.parabola_tangent.step1_d: 🎯 Ek architect parabolic arch design kar raha hai aur usay support beam angle calculate karne ke liye ek point par tangent line find karni hai. Line equation y = mx + c ko parabola y² = 4ax mein substitute karein.
theorem.class12.parabola_tangent.step2_d: 📐 Expand karein: m²x² + 2mcx + c² = 4ax. Rearrange karein: m²x² + 2(mc−2a)x + c² = 0. Yeh x mein ek quadratic hai.
theorem.class12.parabola_tangent.step3_d: 🧮 Tangency ke liye, line theek ek point par intersect karni chahiye. Iska matlab hai quadratic ki ek double root hai: iska discriminant D = b²−4ac zero hona chahiye.
theorem.class12.parabola_tangent.step4_d: ✅ Expand karein: 4(m²c²−4amc+4a²) − 4m²c² = 0 → −16amc + 16a² = 0 → mc = a → c = a/m. Tangent equation slope form mein: y = mx + a/m. Yeh formula architects ko parabolic arch par kisi bhi point par support beam angle find karne mein madad karta hai!
theorem.class12.parabola_tangent.practic: Parabola y² = 8x. Line slope m = 2. Tangency ke liye c find karein (c = a/m).
theorem.class12.ellipse_standard.title: Ellipse ki Standard Equation
theorem.class12.ellipse_standard.finalFo: Ek ellipse jiske foci (±c, 0) par hon jahan c² = a²−b².
theorem.class12.ellipse_standard.keyInsi: Ellipse un points ka set hai jahan do foci ke distances ka sum constant (2a) hota hai. Yehi wajah hai ke planetary orbits elliptical hain jahan Sun ek focus par hota hai (Kepler’s First Law). Flattening eccentricity e = c/a = √(1−b²/a²) par depend karti hai!
theorem.class12.ellipse_standard.step1_l: Definition: foci ke distances ka sum = 2a
theorem.class12.ellipse_standard.step2_l: Foci ko (±c, 0) aur point P(x, y) par set karein
theorem.class12.ellipse_standard.step3_l: Isolate aur do baar square karein
theorem.class12.ellipse_standard.step4_l: Standard form pane ke liye b² = a²−c² lein
theorem.class12.ellipse_standard.step1_d: 🌍 Johannes Kepler ne daryaft kiya ke planets Sun ke gird elliptical paths mein ghoomtay hain. Sun ek focus par hota hai. Ellipse par kisi bhi point se do foci ke darmiyan distances ka sum constant hota hai — major axis ki length.
theorem.class12.ellipse_standard.step2_d: 📐 Foci F₁(−c, 0) aur F₂(c, 0) par rakhein. Ellipse par kisi bhi point P(x,y) ke liye: √[(x+c)²+y²] + √[(x−c)²+y²] = 2a.
theorem.class12.ellipse_standard.step3_d: 🧮 Ek radical isolate karein, dono sides ka square lein, phir bacha hua radical isolate karein aur dobara square lein. Simplify karne ke baad: (a²−c²)x² + a²y² = a²(a²−c²).
theorem.class12.ellipse_standard.step4_d: ✅ Defina karein b² = a²−c². Phir b²x² + a²y² = a²b² → x²/a² + y²/b² = 1. Earth's orbit ke liye: a ≈ 149.6 million km, b ≈ 149.5 million km (taqreeban circular, e ≈ 0.017). Mars ki eccentricity e ≈ 0.093 hai — wazeh tor par zyada elliptical!
theorem.class12.ellipse_standard.practic: Ellipse x²/25 + y²/9 = 1. c (center se focus tak distance) find karein.
theorem.class12.hyperbola_standard.title: Hyperbola ki Standard Equation
theorem.class12.hyperbola_standard.final: Ek hyperbola jiske foci (±c, 0) par hon jahan c² = a²+b².
theorem.class12.hyperbola_standard.keyIn: Hyperbola un points ka set hai jahan do foci ke distances ka difference constant (2a) hota hai. Ellipse (sum constant) ke baraks, hyperbola ki do alag branches hoti hain. Yeh navigation (LORAN systems) mein use hota hai jahan do stations se signals pohnchne ke time ka difference ek ship ko locate karta hai!
theorem.class12.hyperbola_standard.step1: Definition: foci ke distances ka difference = 2a
theorem.class12.hyperbola_standard.step2: Foci ko (±c, 0) aur point P(x, y) par set karein
theorem.class12.hyperbola_standard.step3: Do baar square karein (ellipse jaisa process lekin sign mukhtalif hai)
theorem.class12.hyperbola_standard.step4: Standard form pane ke liye b² = c²−a² lein
theorem.class12.hyperbola_standard.step1: 🚢 Ek ship LORAN navigation use karti hai: F₁ aur F₂ par do radio stations synchronized signals bhejte hain. Ship arrival times mein difference measure karti hai. Constant time difference ka locus ek hyperbola hai jiske foci stations par hain.
theorem.class12.hyperbola_standard.step2: 📐 F₁(−c,0) aur F₂(c,0) tak distances ka absolute difference 2a ke barabar hota hai. Woh branch choose karein jahan PF₁ > PF₂ ho.
theorem.class12.hyperbola_standard.step3: 🧮 Ellipse wali double-squaring technique use karein. Lekin sign ka farq (subtraction vs addition) ek mukhtalif final form tak le jata hai.
theorem.class12.hyperbola_standard.step4: ✅ Define karein b² = c²−a². Standard form x²/a² − y²/b² = 1 ki do branches hoti hain (left aur right). Asymptotes: y = ±(b/a)x. LORAN ship: agar stations 100km door hain (2c=100) aur constant difference 60km hai (2a=60), toh b² = 50²−30² = 1600, aur ship x²/900 − y²/1600 = 1 par hai!
theorem.class12.hyperbola_standard.pract: Hyperbola x²/16 − y²/9 = 1. c (focus distance from center) find karein.
